headline: Tropical Cyclone GEZANI-26 at Red Alert — 69 Active Events Across 33 Countries
summary: The global disaster landscape is dominated by widespread wildfire activity across Sub-Saharan Africa, with 43 active fires. Tropical Cyclone GEZANI-26 remains the most critical event, affecting 1.754 million people in Madagascar and Mozambique. Long-duration droughts continue in Brazil, East Africa, and Central-South Asia.
keyInsights: Critical alert: Tropical Cyclone in Madagascar, Mozambique with 1.754 Million population exposed.
Wildfire activity dominates with 43 events, concentrated in Central African Republic (19 events) and Sudan.
Long-duration drought conditions persist across 3 regions at elevated (orange) alert level, spanning multiple continents.
Monitoring 33 countries across 26 distinct activity days. Most recent events recorded on 2026-02-10.
Flood events across 9 countries have displaced approximately 60,536 people.
